key_observations: Strong return to consistent training after 2-week gap (May 25–Jun 08): only single runs of 6–9.5km indicate recovery or base-building phase
Significant volume and intensity jump in final 2 weeks: doubled run frequency (1→3 runs, then 2 runs) with 15+ km weeks and HR elevation from 158→166 bpm signals fitness gains but compressed timeline
Pace stability excellent across 8 weeks (5:39–6:14 min/km runs): consistent aerobic efficiency despite HR rise suggests improved economy or true fitness progression
warnings: Rapid training load increase over 14 days (6.3km week to 15.7km week) without gradual progression—overtraining risk if sustained without recovery week
HR data only available for final 2 weeks; cannot validate aerobic adaptation across full 8-week block or assess baseline drift
recommendations: Insert a recovery week (8–10km, single or 2 easy runs max) after week of 2026-06-29 to consolidate fitness gains and mitigate fatigue accumulation
Establish consistent HR monitoring zones immediately; current 158–167 bpm range suggests Zone 2–3 training—structure runs by intensity (Z2 easy + 1 Z3/threshold session weekly)
Maintain 1 rest day minimum between runs; Friday football + Monday run (June 27–30) shows 48-hour recovery only—extend to 72 hours given recent volume spike

weeks_analysis.assessment: Minimal baseline week; likely returning from extended break or injury. Single 5.3km run at easy pace (5:39).
Aggressive 158% jump in volume. Long steady run (13.67km @ 6:14) suggests aerobic base work. No data loss apparent; standard buildup pattern.
2-week gap in data (May 18–24 unreported). Volume reduction to 9.5km; likely planned drop or forced rest. Pace maintained (6:11).
Conservative week post-gap: 7.2km single run. Pace consistent (6:12). Suggests cautious return or recovery microcycle.
Lowest volume in tracking period (6.3km). Single run at faster pace (5:47). May indicate tapering or low-commitment week before intensity block.
Major jump: 3 runs, 15.7km, HR baseline established (158 bpm avg). Includes football (cross-training, 158 bpm). Marks transition to structured intensity phase.
Sustained high volume (2 runs, 15.1km) with HR elevation +8 bpm (158→166). Pace remains efficient (5:58–5:59). Clear fitness gain; fatigue accumulating.
